titleStmt.title: SII 28.121: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 121. (A.R. No. 276 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, First prākāra, south wall.

Records a royal order, conveyed through Vaṅgattaraiyaṉ (Vaṅgattaraiyaṉōlai) addressed to the various authorities in the temple of God Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyār to have inscribed in the Vikkirama-Chōḻaṉtirumāḷigai, the record of the setting up of a garden named Ulagai-muḻudumuḍaiyāḷ-tirunandavanam in which plantain trees and flower plants were to be grown to supply flowers for the tiruppū-maṇḍapam of the God (mudaliyār) and Goddess (Nāchchiyār). The land for the purpose amounting to 1440 kuḻi is stated to have been taken on three locations. The plots concerned seem to have been already in the enjoyment of the temple and hence some other land had been given in exchange (talai-māṟu) for securing the execution of the purpose for which the land taken away was earmarked. In this context several names of geographical features, ūr, naḍai-tōppu, etc., are mentioned.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . śrīmakkittikku mel komāṟa paṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikkirama pāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu Āṟāvatu nāḷ 3 100 5 10 5ṉāl vaṅkattaraiyaṉ Olai Uṭaiyār tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyār koyiṟ kaṇkāṇi karaṇattārum tirukkaioṭṭip paṇṭārattārum kaṇṭu mutaliyārkkum nācciyārkkum cāttiyaruḷat tiruppū maṇṭapattukku mutalāka
tiruppaḷḷittāmam Aḷakka nampirāṭṭiyār Ulaka muḻutuṭaiyār tirunāmattāṟ ceyta Irājākkaḷ nāyaṉ tiruttoppukkut teṟkil Ulakamuḻatuṭaiyār naṭaikkut teṟku Āḷiyār tirunantavaṉattukku pratikṣetramākap paḷḷipaṭaiyāṉa vikkirama coḻanallūril tillaivaṉamuṭaiya parameśvara tirunāmattuk kāṇiyāy vilaikoṇṭuviṭṭa nilam (1/4)mā ṉāl kuḻi
7 100 6 10 8m viṭṭu Itukkuttalaimāṟu Āḷiyār tirunantavaṉamāṉa Ulakamuḻutuṭaiyāṉ tirunantavaṉamum Erukkāṭṭañceriyāṉa jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻanallūril taṇṭalaṅ kiḻavaṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ pakkal vilaikoṇṭa cuntaracoḻavatikkuk kiḻakku Irācarācaṉ vāykkālukku vaṭakku tat� tirucciṟṟampala viḷākam nilattukkut teṟku
māṅkollai vāykkālukku vaṭakku pirāṉār nilattukkuk kiḻakku tillainācciyār tirunāmattukkāṇi Iḷamai nayāka viḷākam koḻukkuttukku teṟkum Iṉṉāṉkellaiyuṭpaṭṭa kuḻi 2 100m Itaṉ teṟku māṅkollai Eṉṟu peruṭaiya nilamāy tirunantavaṉañ ceyta kuḻi 6 100 [�10] kkum talaimāṟu jivitakāṟaṟkku jivitamaṭaitta perumpaṟṟappu
liyūṟ piṭākai maṇalūrāṉa jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻanallūril śrīkuntavatevivatikku meṟku Irācarācaṉ vāykkālukkut teṟku 2kū3 � veḷḷaṟai kavuciyaṉ pāṇṭaṉ mātevaṉ pakkal vilaikoṇṭa koyilāṉ ceyyāṉa kaṟpaka viḷākattukku Ellai poṟiyātā nilattukkuk kiḻakku Uṭaiyār tiruppuliśvaramuṭaiyār tiru
nāmattukkāṇikkut teṟkuk kaṇṇāṟṟu vāykkālukku meṟku kāri tevaṉ piḷḷaiyuḷḷiṭṭār nilattukku vaṭakku Iṉṉāṉkellaiyuṭpaṭṭa » kuḻi [6 100] Iraṇṭumā 10m viṭṭu Itukkut talaimāṟu Inta māṅkollai kuḻi 600 �10 Ākakkuḻi 9 1000 4 100 4 10m Ulakaimuḻutuṭaiyāḷ tirunantavaṉameṉṉun tirunāmattāl tirunantavaṉañ ceytu
1. In the top right hand corner there is a fragmentary inscription in characters on the 12th century running as follows:—

ṇṭalattu koṭṭattup paricāla nāṭṭu
kupparuppu Irukāḻiyum kuḻai kummāyattukkuppa paru
ṉ kuḻittaḷikaiyil Ama vikkiramacoḻa
nāḻiyum kaṟiyamutukku muṉṟu paya peramatu
ritūṇi ṉā nāḻiyum Āka nāḷ Oṉṟukku kkupponaka
ḻi muvuḻakkum tiṅkaṭ tirunāṭ(kaḷukkum Āṭ)ṭait tirunāṭkaḷuk
vikkiramacoḻaṉ
vāḻa maraṅkaḷum pūmaraṅkaḷumākkat tirunantavaṉañ ceytu tiruppū maṇṭapattukku mutalākat tiruppaḷḷittāmam A
ḷakkavum Ippaṭikku vikkiramacoḻaṉ tirumāḷikaiyile kalveṭṭavum paṇṇuvate § Ippaṭi ceyyap paṇṇuvate §
Ivai vaṅkattaraiyaṉ Eḻuttu ḥ—
Digital edition of SII 28.121 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
